source_of_truth: docs/feature/client-driven-onboarding/distill/roadmap.json (CDO-S4 entry) + ADR-050 (§a reissue dual emission, §d mode discovery, §e KPI fallout / AR-5) + ADR-048 (§3 auth-proxy local routes, §5 observability events) + ui-cookie-session delta-and-decisions.md (§3 D1 cookie attributes, §6 D8 un-park, UC-6 never-collapse)
paradigm: object-oriented (default per CLAUDE.md absence). NOTE: auth-proxy is functional TypeScript (Hono app, module-scoped functions, no domain classes) — the crafter writes idiomatic functional TS matching the surrounding file, example-based vitest. No XState here.
scope_boundary: auth-proxy/ ONLY (app.ts + its vitest test files; lib/cookies.ts reused unchanged). NON-GOALS (CDO-S5): WorkOS org-create interception, lib/org-create-workflow.ts, x-provisioned-org-id strip-list, ui/ surfaces (login.tsx mode consumption, onboarding-driver), session-chat egress, compose env deltas + override AUTH_MODE pin deletion. The execution-log.json lives at the deliver root (shared with CDO-S1/S2/S3); CDO-S4 appends step ids 04-01..04-03.
binding_amendments: ADR-050 §a: the reissue hook is MODE-AGNOSTIC — emission is path/method/status-guarded ONLY (POST /api/orgs → 201, user token). Do NOT gate emission on AUTH_MODE != dev. Only the `Secure` cookie ATTRIBUTE is dev-gated (omit Secure when AUTH_MODE == 'dev', as the callback handler does at app.ts:178). This is the documented HIGH risk note in the DISTILL CDO-S4 entry.

ADR-050 §a + ui-cookie-session UC-6: the two Set-Cookie headers (auth_token + session) are NEVER collapsed into one comma-joined header — append each buildSetCookie result as its own header (the { append: true } pattern the callback uses at app.ts:179-198). The X-New-Access-Token / X-New-Token-Expires-In header pair is RETAINED alongside (dual emission); D2/D9 unchanged.

ADR-050 §d: GET /api/auth/config is side-effect-free — it MUST NOT mint or remember a CSRF state (that is why it is NOT folded into /api/auth/login, which mints a one-shot state per call). No credential required; registered BEFORE the catch-all app.all('*') per the /api/auth/me pattern; Cache-Control: public, max-age=300. auth-proxy is the SOLE AUTH_MODE reader.

ADR-050 §e / AR-5: the auth_retry_clicked inbound KPI trigger (app.ts ~703-709) RETIRES — retry_clicked died with the CDO-S3 closed union, and the retry funnel re-derives from org_create.intercepted (lands in CDO-S5). Remove the dead inbound-event-type trigger + its app.test.ts coverage. The error_recoverable→auth_recoverable_error_shown and ready→ready_reached emitters STAY (they read the upstream /state projection, not the inbound event).
gate_command: cd auth-proxy && SKIP_DOCKER_ACCEPTANCE=1 npx vitest run && npx tsc --noEmit
gate_note: IMPORTANT (gate gap): the refinery --auto gate does NOT run auth-proxy vitest (content-aware --auto routes auth-proxy diffs to the backend selector). The local `cd auth-proxy && SKIP_DOCKER_ACCEPTANCE=1 npx vitest run` green run is the ONLY pre-merge verification — MANDATORY before submit. SKIP_DOCKER_ACCEPTANCE=1 keeps the [live] multi-replica suite skipped (it un-skips only when the dashboard-chat/auth-proxy:bazel image is loaded; that tag is absent, but the env var is belt-and-braces). node_modules is symlinked from the main checkout — DO NOT npm install.
acceptance_command: cd tests/acceptance/org-onboarding && uv run --no-project pytest -m cdo_s4
acceptance_outer_loop: tests/acceptance/org-onboarding/ (cdo_s4 marker) is the RED outer loop, run by the orchestrator at the post-merge integration gate against the real compose stack (NOT per-step). Driving tests: test_mode_discovery.py (GET /api/auth/config dev contract + cacheable + side-effect-free), test_reissue_sets_cookie.py (Set-Cookie auth_token HttpOnly on POST /api/orgs 201 + Phase-D continuity with the same bearer). The workos-mode arm of mode discovery + the workos claim-correctness of the reissue are COMPOSE-GATED (auth-proxy unit only; the dev acceptance stack cannot run in workos mode — DWD-6). The KPI-trigger removal has NO acceptance scenario; its verifier is the app.test.ts unit suite.
stack_note: No compose stack running; host ports free (project defaults to 'doorman'). Bring up THIS worktree's stack: docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev-no-org.yml up -d api auth-proxy ui-state redis. REBUILD the auth-proxy image after the code change before the acceptance run: ... up -d --build auth-proxy. Suite env (README + conftest): AUTH_MODE=dev + DEV_NO_ORG=true; auth-proxy ingress on :1042; REVERSE_PROXY routes /api + /ui-state. Done → docker compose down (leave images).
must_not_regress: CDO-S1 walking skeleton + the 5 cdo_s1 acceptance checkpoints (HTTP) — auth-proxy change must not break the dev happy path.
CDO-S2 backend gate (no backend files touched this slice).
CDO-S3 closed-union ui-state behaviour (no ui-state/shared files touched).

The full auth-proxy vitest suite — the ui-cookie-session callback Set-Cookie tests, the /api/auth/me + logout tests, the header>cookie credential-read tests, the org-create reissue header tests, and the OTHER two KPI emitters (auth_recoverable_error_shown, ready_reached). Net test changes track retired/added contracts 1:1; NO .skip/.only/xit; Iron Rule on surviving assertions.
The retained X-New-Access-Token / X-New-Token-Expires-In reissue header contract + the R7 backend-cannot-smuggle security tests in org-create-reissue.test.ts.

phases.name: CDO-S4 auth-proxy mode discovery + reissue Set-Cookie (D8 un-park) + retry-KPI cleanup

steps.name: GET /api/auth/config mode discovery (side-effect-free, no credential, cacheable)
steps.criteria: Register a NEW local auth-proxy route GET /api/auth/config returning 200 {"mode": "dev" | "workos"} where mode = (process.env.AUTH_MODE || "dev") === "dev" ? "dev" : "workos" (auth-proxy is the SOLE AUTH_MODE reader). Requirements: (1) registered BEFORE the catch-all app.all('*') — place it next to GET /api/auth/me (~app.ts:283-298), which is the established pattern for a locally-served /api/auth/* route that must NOT be proxied to the backend; (2) requires NO credential (no readCredential, no 401 — it is pre-auth by definition, the login surface calls it before any sign-in); (3) SIDE-EFFECT-FREE — it MUST NOT mint or remember a CSRF state (do not call rememberLoginState; that is exactly why §d keeps it separate from /api/auth/login which mints a one-shot state per call); (4) sets Cache-Control: public, max-age=300. A second identical call returns the same answer (no state mutated).
files_to_modify: auth-proxy/app.ts — add `app.get('/api/auth/config', (c) => { ... })` registered before `app.all('*')` (co-locate with /api/auth/me). Body: read mode from AUTH_MODE (default 'dev'), set Cache-Control: public, max-age=300 via c.header(...), return c.json({ mode }). No credential read, no CSRF state mint, no upstream proxy. A short comment citing ADR-050 §d (side-effect-free; SOLE AUTH_MODE reader; pre-auth; cacheable).

auth-proxy/app.test.ts — RED_UNIT driving cases in a new describe block 'mode discovery: GET /api/auth/config (ADR-050 §d)': (a) AUTH_MODE=dev → 200 {mode:'dev'} + Cache-Control contains 'max-age=300' (or at least 'max-age'); (b) AUTH_MODE=workos → 200 {mode:'workos'} (the compose-gated workos arm covered here at the unit level per DWD-6); (c) no credential required (a bare request with no Authorization/Cookie still 200, NOT 401); (d) side-effect-free: two calls return identical bodies AND a prior GET /api/auth/login (workos mode) state count / behaviour is unaffected — assert config does not consume/mint a login state (e.g. call config, then a workos callback with a state minted by /api/auth/login still validates — or more simply assert config never calls the login-state path: two config calls both 200 with no Set-Cookie and no state side effect). Mirror the env save/restore + mockFetch patterns already in app.test.ts (set process.env.AUTH_MODE per case, restore in afterEach).

auth-proxy/lib/openapi.ts — OPTIONAL: only if the project's openapi.test.ts asserts route coverage and would otherwise break; otherwise DO NOT touch (config is a discovery affordance, not part of the token/PAT lifecycle the openapi doc covers). GREP first; leave untouched if not required.
steps.implementation_notes: Pure additive route. The acceptance test (test_mode_discovery.py, cdo_s4 marker) goes GREEN at the post-merge gate against the dev stack. Keep the handler tiny and synchronous — no async, no fetch. Cache-Control header: use c.header('Cache-Control', 'public, max-age=300'). Do NOT add it to isPublicPath / the catch-all — it is served entirely here. Confirm there is no existing /api/auth/config route (grep) before adding.

steps.name: applyOrgCreateReissue: append auth_token + session Set-Cookie pair (D8 un-park), retain headers, mode-agnostic
steps.scenario_name: ADR-050 §a dual emission — POST /api/orgs 201 carries Set-Cookie auth_token (HttpOnly) + session=1 alongside X-New-Access-Token (acceptance: test_reissue_sets_cookie.py::test_org_create_201_reissues_auth_token_cookie)
steps.criteria: Extend applyOrgCreateReissue (app.ts ~839-885) so that, on its EXISTING trigger (POST /api/orgs → 201, computeOrgCreateReissue returns a reissue), it appends — in addition to the retained X-New-Access-Token / X-New-Token-Expires-In headers — TWO distinct Set-Cookie headers built with the SAME buildSetCookie the callback uses (app.ts:179-198, lib/cookies.ts): (1) auth_token=<reissue.token>; HttpOnly; SameSite=Lax; Path=/; Max-Age=<reissue.expiresIn> (+Secure iff AUTH_MODE != 'dev'); (2) the refreshed session flag session=1; SameSite=Lax; Path=/ (+Secure iff AUTH_MODE != 'dev'), NOT HttpOnly. The two Set-Cookie headers are NEVER collapsed (UC-6) — append each as its own header on the returned Response. The hook stays MODE-AGNOSTIC: do NOT gate the cookie emission on AUTH_MODE (the existing path/method/status + isUserToken/baseClaims guards are the only gate); ONLY the Secure attribute is dev-gated. Since applyOrgCreateReissue currently returns `new Response(upstream.body, { status, headers })` with a flat Headers object (which cannot hold two same-named Set-Cookie), use headers.append('Set-Cookie', buildSetCookie(...)) for each cookie (Headers.append preserves duplicates for set-cookie; the Response constructor + Hono fetch emit them as separate headers). ADR-048 §5 observability: where the reissue-emitted log/event records transport, it reports transport: 'both' (only if such an emit already exists in the reissue path — do not invent a new event; GREP for an existing auth.reissue.emitted emitter and update its transport field if present, else SKIP with a note).
files_to_modify: auth-proxy/app.ts — in applyOrgCreateReissue, after computing `reissue` and building the stripped `headers` and setting the two X-New-* headers, append the two Set-Cookie headers: `const secure = (process.env.AUTH_MODE || 'dev') !== 'dev'; headers.append('Set-Cookie', buildSetCookie(COOKIE_AUTH_TOKEN, reissue.token, { httpOnly: true, sameSite: 'Lax', path: '/', maxAge: reissue.expiresIn, secure })); headers.append('Set-Cookie', buildSetCookie(COOKIE_SESSION_FLAG, '1', { sameSite: 'Lax', path: '/', secure }));` (COOKIE_AUTH_TOKEN/COOKIE_SESSION_FLAG/buildSetCookie are already imported at the top of app.ts). Add a comment citing ADR-050 §a / D8 un-park / UC-6. Verify stripReissueHeaders(upstream.headers) returns a mutable Headers that supports append for set-cookie (it does — `new Headers(src)`).

auth-proxy/org-create-reissue.test.ts — RED_UNIT driving cases in a new describe block 'org-create reissue — Set-Cookie dual emission (ADR-050 §a / D8)': use res.headers.getSetCookie() (the Fetch API multi-value accessor, already used by app.test.ts's setCookie helper) to read the Set-Cookie list. Cases: (a) POST /api/orgs 201 → a Set-Cookie auth_token is present, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax, Path=/, Max-Age == reissue expiry, value === the X-New-Access-Token header value (same minted token); (b) a SECOND Set-Cookie session=1 present, NOT HttpOnly; (c) the two are SEPARATE headers (getSetCookie().length >= 2; never one comma-joined value); (d) X-New-Access-Token + X-New-Token-Expires-In STILL present (dual emission, not replacement); (e) dev mode (AUTH_MODE=dev, the suite default) → auth_token cookie has NO Secure attribute; (f) workos mode (AUTH_MODE=workos) → auth_token cookie HAS Secure (mode-agnostic emission, dev-gated Secure only); (g) the does-not-fire cases (POST /api/projects 201, POST /api/orgs 400/409, unauthenticated 401) emit NO auth_token Set-Cookie (the cookie rides the same gate as the header). Reuse/port the parseSetCookie helper shape from app.test.ts if helpful. Mirror the existing issueDevToken/postOrgs/upstreamResponds harness in this file.
auth-proxy/lib/cookies.ts — REUSED UNCHANGED (do NOT modify; buildSetCookie + the cookie-name constants already exist).
steps.implementation_notes: The retained X-New-* header logic is unchanged — only ADD the two cookie appends. Phase D continuity (dev): in dev DEV_NO_ORG resolves the org from the DB by created_by, so the client's next POST /api/projects succeeds with the SAME bearer regardless of the reissue (the acceptance asserts this continuity). Do NOT add cookie logic anywhere except inside applyOrgCreateReissue. The hook only runs on the catch-all branch (app.ts:822) which already verified the user token, so baseClaims/isUserToken gating is intact. Headers.append is the mechanism that lets one Response carry two Set-Cookie headers.

steps.name: Retire the auth_retry_clicked inbound KPI trigger (AR-5; retry_clicked died with the CDO-S3 closed union)
steps.scenario_name: ADR-050 §e / AR-5 — remove the dead auth_retry_clicked trigger (app.ts ~703-709) + its test; the retry funnel re-derives from org_create.intercepted (CDO-S5)
steps.criteria: Remove the auth_retry_clicked KPI emission from emitKpiEventsForResponse (app.ts ~703-709): the `if (inboundEventType === 'retry_clicked') { emitKpiEvent({ event: 'auth_retry_clicked', ... }) }` block. retry_clicked was retired from the wire union in CDO-S3 (closed union), so the trigger is dead code. KEEP the other two emitters: state === 'error_recoverable' → auth_recoverable_error_shown and state === 'ready' → ready_reached (they read the upstream /state projection, NOT the inbound event). If, after removing the only consumer of `inboundEventType`, the `peekInboundEventType` helper + the `const inboundEventType = await peekInboundEventType(...)` call site (app.ts ~634) + the `inboundEventType` parameter of emitKpiEventsForResponse become entirely unreferenced, REMOVE them too (dead-code hygiene); if any other consumer remains, leave them. GREP to confirm before deleting. Update the function/section doc comments that enumerate the three K3 events to enumerate the surviving two.
files_to_modify: auth-proxy/app.ts — delete the auth_retry_clicked branch in emitKpiEventsForResponse; if newly-orphaned, delete peekInboundEventType + its call site + the inboundEventType parameter (collapse emitKpiEventsForResponse to take only `response`); update the docstring above emitKpiEventsForResponse (the 'Events:' list) and the §SD4 comment block (~app.ts:629-634) that mention retry_clicked / auth_retry_clicked. Keep readOnboardingSignal + emitKpiEvent + the two surviving emitters intact.

auth-proxy/app.test.ts — REMOVE the test 'emits auth_retry_clicked when caller forwards a retry_clicked event' (~app.test.ts:179-213) and reconcile the file's header table/comment (rows enumerating auth_retry_clicked) so the suite documents only the surviving emitters. Do NOT weaken or skip the auth_recoverable_error_shown / ready_reached parametrized tests — they stay. This is a SANCTIONED test removal (the behaviour under test was retired upstream, not a failing test being silenced — Iron Rule respected: the test is deleted because its subject no longer exists, with a one-line rationale comment if the describe block remains).
steps.implementation_notes: Pure deletion + doc reconciliation. After this step the full auth-proxy vitest suite must stay GREEN and tsc clean (watch for an unused `peekInboundEventType` / unused parameter triggering noUnusedLocals/noUnusedParameters if those are on — remove cleanly). This is the smallest step; it lands last so the config + reissue commits are independent of the KPI cleanup. No acceptance scenario covers it (KPI emission is stdout observability).

validation.approver: orchestrator (nw-deliver) — grounded in ADR-050 §a/§d/§e + ADR-048 §3/§5 + ui-cookie-session D1/D8/UC-6 + the 2 cdo_s4 acceptance tests (test_mode_discovery.py, test_reissue_sets_cookie.py) + a full read of auth-proxy/app.ts (the live seams: /api/auth/me registration pattern, applyOrgCreateReissue 839-885, buildSetCookie callback usage 173-198, the auth_retry_clicked trigger 703-709), auth-proxy/lib/cookies.ts, and the existing app.test.ts + org-create-reissue.test.ts harnesses
validation.notes: 3 sequential steps, each ending green in the auth-proxy vitest + tsc unit gate. Acceptance (cdo_s4) is the outer RED loop verified at the post-merge integration gate against the real dev compose stack. DISTILL linkage: test_mode_discovery.py → 04-01; test_reissue_sets_cookie.py → 04-02; the KPI-trigger removal (04-03) has no acceptance scenario (its verifier is app.test.ts). Step-id format NN-NN. No new top-level components (additive route + extended hook + deletion; lib/cookies.ts reused). Risk note honored: the reissue hook stays mode-agnostic (only Secure is dev-gated). Gate gap honored: auth-proxy vitest is NOT run by the refinery --auto — the local vitest run is the mandatory pre-merge verification.
